Point in pregnancy: Baseline at conception, with follow-up reported at 6 weeks postpartum
Value threshold or deviation: eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m2<60\ \text{mL/min/1.73 m}^2<60 mL/min/1.73 m2, corresponding to CKD stage 3 to 4. In the cohort, mean eGFR was 71.7 ± 27.6 at conception and 55.8 ± 32.7 at 6 weeks after delivery; the average GFR decline was faster in the <60<60<60 group, -18.8 mL/min overall, stage 3 -20.2, stage 4 -15.8.
Preeclampsia risk / note: Preeclampsia occurred in 36.84% (7/19) with eGFR <60<60<60 versus 6.25% (2/32) with eGFR ≥60\ge 60≥60. 1

Value threshold or deviation: Blood pressure ≥140/90 mmHg\ge 140/90\ \text{mmHg}≥140/90 mmHg on at least 2 occasions, measured 4 hours apart, in a previously normotensive woman. For preeclampsia, this must be accompanied by proteinuria or other maternal organ dysfunction.
Preeclampsia risk / note: This is the positive clinical cutoff used in pregnancy guidance. The sources retrieved here do not give a separate age-specific “normal” pregnancy BP table, the key cutoff is the same 140/90 threshold after 20 weeks. 2
